description: Dr. Anton Maximov delivered a compelling presentation on how artificial intelligence is revolutionizing neuroscience by enabling the analysis of complex brain structures at an unprecedented scale and speed. His work uses AI-driven tools—particularly convolutional neural networks integrated with 3D electron microscopy—to uncover the nanoscale architecture of long-term memory. This research, previously impossible with manual techniques, showcases how AI transforms the pace, precision, and possibility of scientific discovery. Dr. Maximov also reflected on how AI is streamlining everyday scientific tasks, democratizing hypothesis generation, and setting the stage for more dynamic, scalable, and biologically inspired AI systems. His talk illustrated a future where AI not only accelerates research but redefines how science is conceived, conducted, and shared.
summary: Dr. Maximov는 AI가 신경과학을 혁신하여 전례 없는 규모와 속도로 뇌 구조를 분석한다고 발표했습니다. 특히 CNN과 3D 전자 현미경을 활용해 장기 기억의 나노 구조를 밝혀내며 과학 연구의 속도, 정확성, 가능성을 재정의하고 일상 업무를 간소화한다고 강조했습니다. 이는 과학이 수행되는 방식을 재정립합니다.
kor_description:
안톤 막시모프(Anton Maximov) 박사는 인공지능이 전례 없는 규모와 속도로 복잡한 뇌 구조를 분석할 수 있게 함으로써 신경과학을 어떻게 혁신하고 있는지에 대한 매우 흥미로운 발표를 진행했습니다. 그의 연구는 특히 3D 전자현미경과 통합된 컨볼루션 신경망(CNN)과 같은 AI 기반 도구를 활용하여 장기 기억의 나노 규모 아키텍처를 밝혀냈습니다.

수동 기술로는 이전에는 불가능했던 이 연구는 AI가 과학적 발견의 속도, 정밀도, 그리고 가능성을 어떻게 변화시키는지 선명하게 보여주었습니다. 막시모프 박사는 또한 AI가 일상적인 과학 업무를 간소화하고, 가설 생성을 민주화하며, 더욱 역동적이고 확장 가능하며 생물학적으로 영감을 받은 AI 시스템을 위한 기반을 어떻게 마련하고 있는지에 대해서도 고찰했습니다.

그의 강연은 AI가 연구를 가속화할 뿐만 아니라, 과학이 구상되고, 수행되며, 공유되는 방식을 재정의하는 미래를 제시했습니다.

title: Harvard’s AI-Enhanced Classroom: Revolutionizing Learning with Custom GPTs

speakers.introduction: Siya Raj Purohit is an education leader, author, and investor. She works on Education at OpenAI and is a General Partner at Pathway Ventures, an early-stage fund investing in the future of learning and work. Siya was previously an early employee at Udacity and Springboard, an investor at GSV Ventures, and the founding EdTech/Workforce category lead for AWS Marketplace. She is the author of Engineering America, a book on the country's jobs-skills gap
speakers.name: Siya Raj Purohit

speakers.introduction: Jake Cook teaches the popular "Digital Marketing Workshop" course for second-year MBA students at Harvard Business School, covering key areas such as SEO, paid and social media, Amazon strategy, influencer marketing, analytics, and basic machine learning applications with Python and GPT-4. His course also explores emerging trends like generative AI, privacy challenges, and personalization, often enriched by insights from leading industry experts. Learn more about how ChatGPT can be used by students to prove mastering digital marketing concepts and how AI can reduce plagiarism here.
speakers.name: Jake Cook

speakers.introduction: Natalie Cone launched and now manages OpenAI’s interdisciplinary community, the Forum.  The OpenAI Forum is a community designed to unite thoughtful contributors from a diverse array of backgrounds, skill sets, and domain expertise to enable discourse related to the intersection of AI and an array of academic, professional, and societal domains. Before joining OpenAI, Natalie managed and stewarded Scale’s ML/AI community of practice, the AI Exchange. She has a background in the Arts, with a degree in History of Art from UC, Berkeley, and has served as Director of Operations and Programs, as well as on the board of directors for the radical performing arts center, CounterPulse, and led visitor experience at Yerba Buena Center for the Arts.
speakers.name: Natalie Cone

description: The OpenAI Forum hosted an engaging session titled "Harvard’s AI-Enhanced Classroom: Revolutionizing Learning with Custom GPTs", featuring Jake Cook from Harvard Business School and Siya Raj Purohit from OpenAI. Siya provided insights into AI-native universities, showcasing how ChatGPT transforms education at individual, team, and institutional levels. Jake presented real-world applications of AI in his classroom, emphasizing creative experimentation, personalized learning, and the power of play to unlock students' potential. The event highlighted innovative uses of AI, including custom GPTs for dynamic teaching and adaptive learning, fostering both efficiency and critical thinking. The discussion underscored AI's role in reshaping education and left participants with actionable insights and strategies for adoption.
summary: AI 포럼의 "하버드 AI 강화 교실" 세션에서 OpenAI와 하버드 연사는 맞춤형 GPT와 ChatGPT가 교육을 혁신하는 사례를 공유했습니다. 개인화된 학습, 창의적 실험, 역동적인 교수법으로 효율성과 비판적 사고를 높이며 AI의 교육 현장 적용 전략을 제시했습니다.
kor_description:
네, AI Forum 한국인 참가자들에게 핵심 내용이 잘 전달될 수 있도록 번역해 드립니다.

---

**제목: 하버드의 AI 강화 교실: 맞춤형 GPT로 학습 혁신**

OpenAI 포럼은 '하버드의 AI 강화 교실: 맞춤형 GPT로 학습 혁신'이라는 흥미로운 세션을 개최했습니다. 하버드 비즈니스 스쿨의 제이크 쿡(Jake Cook) 교수와 OpenAI의 시야 라지 푸로힛(Siya Raj Purohit)이 연사로 참여했습니다.

시야는 AI 네이티브 대학에 대한 통찰을 제공하며, ChatGPT가 개인, 팀, 그리고 기관 수준에서 교육을 어떻게 변화시키는지 보여주었습니다. 제이크 교수는 자신의 강의실에서 AI를 실제로 적용한 사례들을 제시하며, 창의적인 실험, 개인 맞춤형 학습, 그리고 학생들의 잠재력을 이끌어내는 놀이의 힘을 강조했습니다.

이번 세션은 역동적인 교수법과 적응형 학습을 위한 맞춤형 GPT를 포함한 AI의 혁신적인 활용 사례들을 조명했습니다. 이를 통해 효율성 증진과 비판적 사고 함양이라는 두 가지 목표를 동시에 달성할 수 있음을 보여주었습니다.

이번 논의는 교육의 재편에 있어 AI의 중요한 역할을 다시 한번 강조했으며, 참가자들에게 AI 도입을 위한 실질적인 통찰과 전략을 제공했습니다.

---

title: Collective Alignment: Enabling Democratic Inputs to AI

speakers.introduction: Teddy Lee is a Product Manager at OpenAI on the Collective Alignment Team, which focuses on developing processes and platforms for enabling democratic inputs for steering AI. Previously, Teddy was a founding member of OpenAI’s Human Data team, which focuses on improving OpenAI’s models with human feedback, and has also helped to develop content moderation tooling in the OpenAI API. He has previously held roles at Scale AI, Google, and McKinsey. He serves as the President of the MIT Club of Northern California, the alumni club for 14,000+ Northern California-based MIT alumni, and is a member of the MIT Alumni Association Board of Directors. Teddy holds a BS in Electrical Engineering from Stanford, an MS in Management Science & Engineering from Stanford, and an MBA from MIT Sloan.
speakers.name: Teddy Lee

speakers.introduction: Kevin Feng is a 3rd-year Ph.D. student in Human Centered Design & Engineering at the University of Washington. His research lies at the intersection of social computing and interactive machine learning—specifically, he develops interactive tools and processes to improve the adaptability of large-scale, AI-powered sociotechnical systems. His work has appeared in numerous premier academic venues in human-computer interaction including CHI, CSCW, and FAccT, and has been featured by outlets including UW News and the Montréal AI Ethics Institute. He is the recipient of a 2022 UW Herbold Fellowship. He holds a BSE in Computer Science, with minors in visual arts and technology & society, from Princeton University.
speakers.name: Kevin Feng

speakers.introduction: Founder/Chief Scientist @ Remesh. Working on deliberative alignment for AI and institutions.
speakers.name: Andrew Konya
speakers.position: Cofounder and Chief Scientist, Consultant at UN

description: As AI gets more advanced and widely used, it is essential to involve the public in deciding how AI should behave in order to better align our models to the values of humanity. Last May, we announced the Democratic Inputs to AI grant program. We partnered with 10 teams out of nearly 1000 applicants to design, build, and test ideas that use democratic methods to decide the rules that govern AI systems. Throughout, the teams tackled challenges like recruiting diverse participants across the digital divide, producing a coherent output that represents diverse viewpoints, and designing processes with sufficient transparency to be trusted by the public. At OpenAI, we’re building on this momentum by designing an end-to-end process for collecting inputs from external stakeholders and using those inputs to train and shape the behavior of our models. Our goal is to design systems that incorporate public inputs to steer powerful AI models while addressing the above challenges. To help ensure that we continue to make progress on this research, we have formed a “Collective Alignment” team.
summary: AI가 인간 가치에 부합하도록 대중의 참여를 통한 AI 행동 규칙 결정이 필수적입니다. OpenAI는 'Democratic Inputs to AI' 프로그램을 통해 이를 연구하고 있으며, 다양한 과제를 해결하며 외부 의견을 수집하여 AI 모델 훈련에 반영하는 End-to-End 과정을 구축 중입니다. 이를 위해 'Collective Alignment' 팀을 구성했습니다.
kor_description:
AI가 더욱 고도화되고 광범위하게 사용됨에 따라, 우리의 모델이 인류의 가치에 더 잘 부합하도록 AI의 행동 방식을 결정하는 과정에 대중을 참여시키는 것이 필수적입니다.

지난 5월, 저희는 'AI에 대한 민주적 의견 수렴(Democratic Inputs to AI) 보조금 프로그램'을 발표했습니다. 약 1,000개에 달하는 신청팀 중 10개 팀과 협력하여, AI 시스템의 작동을 규율하는 규칙을 결정하기 위해 민주적인 방식을 활용하는 아이디어를 설계하고 구축하며 테스트했습니다.

이 과정에서 팀들은 디지털 격차를 넘어 다양한 참가자를 모집하는 것, 다양한 관점을 대표하는 일관된 결과물을 도출하는 것, 그리고 대중에게 신뢰받을 수 있을 만큼 충분한 투명성을 갖춘 프로세스를 설계하는 것과 같은 과제들을 해결했습니다.

OpenAI는 이러한 추진력을 바탕으로, 외부 이해관계자로부터 의견을 수집하고 이를 활용하여 모델의 행동을 학습시키고 형성하는 종단 간(end-to-end) 프로세스를 설계하고 있습니다. 우리의 목표는 위에서 언급된 과제들을 해결하면서, 대중의 의견을 반영하여 강력한 AI 모델의 방향을 제시하는 시스템을 설계하는 것입니다. 이 연구에서 지속적인 진전을 이룰 수 있도록, 저희는 "집단 정렬(Collective Alignment)" 팀을 신설했습니다.

title: Fusion Energy: The End of Fossil Fuels

speakers.introduction: Sam Altman is the co-founder and CEO of OpenAI, the AI research and deployment company behind ChatGPT and DALL·E. Sam was president of the early-stage startup accelerator Y Combinator from 2014 to 2019. In 2015, Sam co-founded OpenAI as a nonprofit research lab with the mission to build general-purpose artificial intelligence that benefits all humanity. The company remains governed by the nonprofit and its original charter today.
speakers.name: Sam Altman

speakers.introduction: Dr. David Kirtley is founder and CEO at Helion, a privately funded fusion electricity company creating the world’s first fusion generator. He is passionate about inventing and leading teams to develop disruptive technologies to improve access to energy, reduce carbon emissions, and provide a better future for the following generations. Dr. Kirtley is a fellow of the National Science Foundation, NASA, and the Department of Defense. He is a leading authority on advanced fuel fusion generators, plasma-based in-space propulsion systems, and space reentry architectures. Dr. Kirtley has graduate degrees in Nuclear, Plasma, and Aerospace Engineering from the University of Michigan. He has over 100 publications, 60+ patents and has been pursuing advanced fusion systems since the late 1990s.
speakers.name: David Kirtley

description: Earlier this year, Sam Altman, CEO and Co-Founder of OpenAI and David Kirtley, CEO and Founder of Helion convened at the OpenAI office among a small group of OpenAI Forum members to discuss the future of energy. This is the recording of their discussion.
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman과 Helion CEO David Kirtley가 OpenAI에서 AI 포럼 회원들과 에너지의 미래를 논의했습니다. 이 영상은 당시 토론의 기록입니다.
kor_description: 올해 초, 오픈AI CEO 겸 공동 창립자인 샘 알트만과 헬리온 CEO 겸 창립자인 데이비드 커틀리가 소수의 오픈AI 포럼 회원들과 함께 오픈AI 사무실에 모여 에너지의 미래를 논의했습니다. 다음은 그 논의 내용입니다.

title: Building an AI-Powered University

speakers.introduction: Siya Raj Purohit is an education leader, author, and investor. She works on Education at OpenAI and is a General Partner at Pathway Ventures, an early-stage fund investing in the future of learning and work. Siya was previously an early employee at Udacity and Springboard, an investor at GSV Ventures, and the founding EdTech/Workforce category lead for AWS Marketplace. She is the author of Engineering America, a book on the country's jobs-skills gap
speakers.name: Siya Raj Purohit

speakers.introduction: Jason is a Partner & Co-President at GSV. He has spent the majority of his career as a technology investment banker, specializing in the education and human capital management technology sectors. Throughout his career, Jason has personally executed or advised on a wide range of transactions within growth sub-sectors across the PreK-12, Higher Ed, and enterprise learning and talent continuum. Jason is actively involved across GSV's broader education and talent technology franchise, including the ASU GSV Summit, GSVlabs, and GSV AcceleraTE. Just prior to re-joining GSV Advisors in 2016, Jason led Corporate Development for London-based TES Global (then backed by TPG Capital, now majority owned by Providence Equity Partners). As part of the leadership team at TES Global, Jason helped to transform the organization into a leading digital education business through the execution of nine strategic transactions across the USA, Europe, and Australia.
speakers.name: Jason Horne

speakers.introduction: Dr. Jaci Lindburg has worked in higher education for nearly two decades, serving in a number of key leadership roles that serve to accelerate innovation in postsecondary education. She currently serves jointly as Associate Vice Chancellor of Innovative and Learning-Centric Initiatives at the University of Nebraska at Omaha (UNO), as well as Associate Vice President for Digital Learning & IT Strategy at the University of Nebraska System. As Associate Vice Chancellor, Lindburg leads a unit responsible for uniting the institution’s educational mission, technological capabilities, and student support initiatives to develop and execute digital learning experiences designed to meet the needs of students, faculty, and staff. This includes leading online/hybrid/blended learning, academic technology, workforce development, micro-credentials, experimental pedagogies, lifelong learning, digital badging and various other exploratory pilots. In this role, Dr. Lindburg has envisioned and launched the AI Learning Lab as well as the Center for Competencies, Skills and Workforce Development. Dr. Lindburg also has extensive college teaching experience as affiliate faculty in UNO’s College of Arts & Sciences and UNO’s College of Education, Health and Human Sciences, building curriculum and delivering 45 sections of 17 courses from 2009-2022.
speakers.name: Jaci Jenkins Lindburg

speakers.introduction: Axel Persaud is the Assistant Vice President of Enterprise Engineering and Operations within the Division of IT at the University of Maryland, a position he has held since 2017. He oversees critical areas such as the campus network infrastructure, on-prem and cloud infrastructure, identity management, data warehouse/analytics, and software development, including financial, HR, and student information systems. In addition to his role, Axel co-chaired the University of Maryland’s Presidential Commission on AI and was honored with the President's Distinguished Service Award in 2022 for his outstanding contributions to the university.
speakers.name: Axel Persaud
speakers.position: Assistant Vice President of Enterprise Engineering

speakers.introduction: David Brubaker is the Senior IT Director for AI Initiatives at The Wharton School. David leads efforts to integrate AI tools into Wharton Computing, enhancing learning experiences and driving innovation in education technology. Throughout his 18-year tenure at Penn, David has demonstrated a passion for fostering positive organizational change through relationship building and strategic partnerships. His work reflects a deep commitment to leveraging technology to support the mission of higher education, benefiting individuals, departments, and the University as a whole.
speakers.name: David Brubaker
speakers.position: Senior IT Director for AI Initiatives

speakers.introduction: Leah Belsky is a tech executive and Board Director with over 20 years of experience leading high-growth business and initiatives in the education, tech, and health sectors in both public and private companies. She is currently the GM of Education at OpenAI. Prior to this role Leah was the Chief Revenue Officer at Coursera and is also a Board Director at Oyster.  Leah has been recognized by Fortune Magazine as one of the "40 under 40" leaders in Tech, by Inc Magazine as one of the "15 Top Emerging Executives to Follow," and by GSV Ventures as a "Woman leading the AI revolution in EDU."
speakers.name: Leah Belsky

description:
Welcome to our virtual event, "Building an AI-Powered University," hosted by the OpenAI Forum. The session, moderated by Natalie Cone, OpenAI Forum Community Architect, began with a reminder of OpenAI's mission to ensure that artificial general intelligence (AGI) benefits all of humanity.
The event featured a keynote speech by Leah Belsky, VP of Education at OpenAI. Leah discussed the transformative potential of AI in education, emphasizing the goals of enabling global access to AI for students and teachers, powering AI-driven educational products, and fostering collaboration to enhance AI's educational impact.
The panel included Jason Horn, a technology investment banker and the evening's facilitator, who has a rich background in the education technology sector; Alex Persaud, Assistant Vice President of Enterprise Engineering and Operations at the University of Maryland, who manages critical infrastructure and co-chaired the university’s AI commission; Dr. Jaci Lindberg, who combines roles at the University of Nebraska to push innovation in digital learning and IT strategy; and David Brubaker, who leads AI initiatives at the Wharton School, focusing on aligning AI with educational priorities.
During the discussion, panelists shared their diverse experiences with AI implementation at their institutions. They highlighted the impact of AI tools like ChatGPT in enhancing both the student and faculty experience and improving operational efficiencies across university functions. The session transitioned into a virtual networking event, allowing participants to engage directly with thought leaders and peers interested in AI's future role in education. The event concluded with Natalie Cone encouraging participants to continue the dialogue on AI in education through upcoming OpenAI Forum events and platforms like LinkedIn and Substack, fostering ongoing collaboration and innovation in the field.
summary: "AI 기반 대학 구축" 세션에서 OpenAI는 AI의 교육 혁신 잠재력을 강조했습니다. 패널들은 ChatGPT 등 AI 도구 활용으로 학생 및 교직원 경험과 학사 운영 효율을 높이는 방안을 공유하며, 교육 분야 AI 협력의 중요성을 논의했습니다.
kor_description:
OpenAI 포럼이 주최하는 가상 행사 'AI 기반 대학 구축'에 오신 것을 환영합니다. OpenAI 포럼 커뮤니티 아키텍트인 내털리 콘이 진행한 이번 세션은 범용 인공지능(AGI)이 모든 인류에게 이로움을 줄 수 있도록 하겠다는 OpenAI의 사명을 다시 한번 강조하며 시작되었습니다.

이번 행사에서는 OpenAI 교육 부문 부사장인 리아 벨스키의 기조연설이 있었습니다. 리아는 교육 분야에서 AI의 혁신적인 잠재력에 대해 논하며, 학생과 교사를 위한 AI의 전 세계적 접근성 확보, AI 기반 교육 제품 개발, 그리고 AI의 교육적 영향력 강화를 위한 협력 증진이라는 목표를 강조했습니다.

패널로는 교육 기술 분야에서 풍부한 경험을 가진 기술 투자 은행가이자 이번 행사의 진행자인 제이슨 혼; 메릴랜드 대학교의 엔터프라이즈 엔지니어링 및 운영 담당 부조교수로서 핵심 인프라를 관리하고 대학 AI 위원회 공동 위원장을 역임한 알렉스 퍼소드; 네브래스카 대학교에서 디지털 학습 및 IT 전략 혁신을 주도하는 재시 린드버그 박사; 그리고 와튼 스쿨에서 AI 이니셔티브를 이끌며 AI와 교육적 우선순위의 연계에 집중하는 데이비드 브루베이커가 참여했습니다.

토론 시간 동안 패널들은 각 기관에서 AI를 구현한 다양한 경험을 공유했습니다. 이들은 ChatGPT와 같은 AI 도구가 학생 및 교직원 경험을 향상하고 대학 기능 전반의 운영 효율성을 개선하는 데 미치는 영향을 강조했습니다.

세션은 가상 네트워킹 행사로 이어져, 참가자들이 AI의 교육 분야 미래 역할에 관심을 가진 사고 리더 및 동료들과 직접 교류할 수 있도록 했습니다. 내털리 콘은 다가오는 OpenAI 포럼 행사 및 링크드인, 서브스택과 같은 플랫폼을 통해 교육 분야 AI에 대한 대화를 지속할 것을 참가자들에게 독려하며, 이 분야의 지속적인 협력과 혁신을 촉진했습니다.

title: Building an AI-Ready Workforce: A Conversation with Randi Weingarten and Sean McGarvey

speakers.introduction: Ann M. O’Leary is the Vice President of Global Policy at Open AI, where she leads high impact policy strategy at the nexus of technology and governance. A seasoned lawyer and policy leader, she spent decades shaping legislative and regulatory outcomes across state, federal, and global arenas to advance policies that promote growth and enable responsible innovation.
speakers.name: Ann O'Leary

description:
AI is beginning to reshape how people work across industries. OpenAI's Vice President of Global Policy Ann O'Leary sat down for a conversation with Randi Weingarten, President of the American Federation of Teachers, and Sean McGarvey, President of North America's Building Trades Union (NABTU) about how workers and institutions are responding to that shift, and what it will take to prepare millions of Americans for an AI-driven economy.
The conversation focused on the importance of moving from resistance to adoption, with both leaders emphasizing training, certification, and lifelong learning as critical tools for helping workers adapt. They highlighted the growing demand for accessible AI education and the role trusted institutions can play in guiding workers through this transition. At the same time, the discussion underscored the need for stronger coordination across the public and private sectors. As AI accelerates change, investments in reskilling, clear guardrails, and policies that address displacement will be essential to ensure workers can adapt and continue to thrive.
summary: AI 시대 노동 변화에 대비, OpenAI, 교사 노조, 건설 노조 리더들이 논의했습니다. 이들은 AI 채택을 위해 교육, 평생 학습, 자격증이 필수적이며, 대중의 AI 교육 접근성 확대, 공공-민간 협력, 재교육 투자, 정책적 안전장치 마련이 시급함을 강조했습니다.
kor_description:
AI는 산업 전반에 걸쳐 사람들이 일하는 방식을 재편하기 시작했습니다. OpenAI의 글로벌 정책 부사장 Ann O'Leary는 미국교원연맹 회장 Randi Weingarten, 북미건설노조(NABTU) 회장 Sean McGarvey와 함께, 노동자들과 기관들이 이러한 변화에 어떻게 대응하고 있으며, 수백만 미국인들이 AI 기반 경제에 대비하기 위해 무엇이 필요한지에 대해 대화를 나누었습니다.

대화는 저항에서 수용으로 나아가는 것의 중요성에 초점을 맞췄으며, 두 리더는 노동자들이 적응하는 데 필수적인 도구로 훈련, 자격증 취득, 평생 학습을 강조했습니다. 그들은 접근 가능한 AI 교육에 대한 증가하는 수요와 신뢰할 수 있는 기관이 노동자들을 이 전환 과정으로 안내하는 데 있어 수행할 수 있는 역할을 역설했습니다. 동시에, 이번 논의는 공공 및 민간 부문 간의 강력한 협력 필요성을 강조했습니다. AI가 변화를 가속화함에 따라, 노동자들이 적응하고 계속해서 번성할 수 있도록 재교육, 명확한 안전장치, 그리고 직업 상실을 다루는 정책에 대한 투자가 필수적일 것입니다.

title: Lowering Barriers to AI Adoption

speakers.introduction: Conor Grennan is Chief AI Architect at NYU Stern School of Business, where he builds generative AI fluency across the institution, including MBAs, faculty and administration. He is also CEO and Founder of AI Mindset, an AI consulting company that trains professionals, leaders and organizations on a new and effective framework for generative AI. He has worked with teams across industry, including at McKinsey, NASA, PWC, ServiceNow, JP Morgan, DaVita Healthcare, Pfizer, NYU Langone, Providence Health, American Securities, and more.
speakers.name: Conor Grennan

speakers.introduction: Matt is the first appointed Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer of a private equity-backed life sciences consulting firm, Inizio, where he leads enterprise transformation, product development, customer engagement, staff upskilling , innovation, research, publication and partnership efforts both internally across this 10,000+ Company, and in collaboration with many leading health and biopharmaceutical, biotech, medtech, Healthtech and neurotech companies. Matt is the Co-Chair of the International Society for Medical Publications Professionals Artificial Intelligence Task Force, a Founding Board Member of the Foundation for Artificial Intelligence and Health, and CEO of LLMental, a venture studio for AI-powered mental wellness.
speakers.name: Matt Lewis
speakers.position: Global Chief Artificial and Augmented Intelligence Officer

description: Conor provided a practical framework for rethinking how we use large language models like ChatGPT, emphasizing that the key to effective implementation is about changing behavior rather than just learning new technology. He presented a three-part framework that aligns with how we actually work: Learn, Execute, and Strategize. This approach promised to elevate generative AI from an intermittently-used tool to a transformative force that could dramatically improve decision-making, efficiency, and strategic planning. Conor spoke on topics such as Not Your Typical Tech Upgrade: Why our brains resist AI, Breaking Bad Habits: Shifting away from Google-centric thinking, The AI Mindset: Embracing a new paradigm for interaction, Hands-on Transformation: Live demo of AI in action, and Beyond Text: Exploring AI's expanding capabilities.
summary: Conor는 LLM 활용의 핵심이 행동 변화에 있음을 강조하며, 업무에 맞춰 '학습, 실행, 전략화' 3단계 프레임워크를 제안했습니다. 이 접근법은 AI를 의사결정, 효율, 전략 기획을 혁신하는 핵심 동력으로 격상시킬 것입니다.
kor_description: 코너는 ChatGPT와 같은 대규모 언어 모델을 활용하는 방식에 대한 실용적인 프레임워크를 제시하며, 효과적인 구현의 핵심이 단순히 새로운 기술을 배우는 것이 아니라 행동을 변화시키는 데 있다고 강조했습니다. 그는 우리가 실제로 일하는 방식과 일치하는 세 가지 부분으로 구성된 프레임워크, 즉 학습(Learn), 실행(Execute), 전략 수립(Strategize)을 소개했습니다. 이 접근 방식은 생성형 AI를 간헐적으로 사용되는 도구에서 의사 결정, 효율성, 전략 기획을 획기적으로 개선할 수 있는 혁신적인 힘으로 끌어올릴 것을 약속했습니다. 코너는 다음과 같은 주제들을 다루었습니다: 평범한 기술 업그레이드가 아닙니다: 왜 우리 뇌는 AI에 저항하는가, 나쁜 습관 깨기: 구글 중심적 사고에서 벗어나기, AI 마인드셋: 새로운 상호작용 패러다임 수용하기, 실습을 통한 변화: AI 작동 라이브 데모, 그리고 텍스트를 넘어서: AI의 확장되는 역량 탐구.

title: Enabling a Data Driven Workforce

speakers.introduction: Lois Newman is focused on Customer Education at OpenAI, specializing in user education and AI adoption. With over 10 years of experience in SaaS, she has extensive experience in developing and delivering engaging content, from large-scale webinars to stage presentations, aimed at enhancing user understanding and adoption of new technologies. Lois works closely with customers to ensure ChatGPT is integrated into daily activities and effectively utilized in the workplace. Lois is known for her storytelling approach, making complex technology relatable and accessible to all audiences.
speakers.name: Lois Newman

speakers.introduction: Aaron Wilkowitz is a Sales Engineer at OpenAI focused on the US Federal Government and other Public Sector accounts.
speakers.name: Aaron Wilkowitz

description:
The webinar, part of the ongoing ChatGPT Enterprise Learning Lab series, featured Ben Kinsella, a member of OpenAI’s Human Data Team, alongside Lois Newman, Customer Success Manager, and Aaron Wilkowitz, Solutions Engineer. They explored how ChatGPT Enterprise can empower organizations by streamlining data analysis, enhancing productivity, and fostering a data-driven culture.
Key Takeaways:
AI-Powered Data Analysis:
Q&A Highlights:
Elan Weiner, Solutions Engineer, joined for a live Q&A, answering questions about integrating ChatGPT into organizational workflows and data security concerns.
summary: OpenAI 팀은 ChatGPT Enterprise가 데이터 분석 간소화, 생산성 향상, 데이터 중심 문화 조성을 통해 조직을 어떻게 강화하는지 소개했습니다. 세션에서는 통합 및 데이터 보안 관련 질문에도 답변하며 기업 활용 방안을 제시했습니다.
kor_description:
진행 중인 ChatGPT 엔터프라이즈 학습 랩 시리즈의 일환으로 개최된 이번 웹 세미나에는 OpenAI의 휴먼 데이터 팀원인 벤 킨셀라, 고객 성공 매니저 로이스 뉴먼, 솔루션 엔지니어 아론 윌코위츠가 참여했습니다. 이들은 ChatGPT 엔터프라이즈가 데이터 분석을 간소화하고, 생산성을 향상시키며, 데이터 기반 문화를 조성함으로써 조직의 역량을 어떻게 강화할 수 있는지에 대해 논의했습니다.

**주요 내용:**
AI 기반 데이터 분석:

**Q&A 주요 내용:**
솔루션 엔지니어 엘란 와이너가 라이브 Q&A 세션에 합류하여 ChatGPT를 조직 워크플로우에 통합하는 방법과 데이터 보안 문제에 대한 질문에 답변했습니다.

title: AI Art From the Uncanny Valley to Prompting: Gains and Losses

speakers.introduction: Dr. Ahmed Elgammal is a professor at the Department of Computer Science and an Executive Council Faculty at the Center for Cognitive Science at Rutgers University. He is the founder and director of the Art and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory at Rutgers. Dr. Elgammal has published over 200 peer-reviewed papers, book chapters, and books in the fields of computer vision, machine learning and digital humanities. Dr. Elgammal is also the founder of Playform AI, a platform for making AI accessible for artists. Dr. Elgammal research on knowledge discovery in art history, AI-art generation, and AI-based at authentication received global media attention, including several reports on the Washington Post, New York Times, CNN, NBC, CBS News, Science News, and many others. In 2016, a TV segment about his research, produced for PBS, won an Emmy award. In 2017, he developed AICAN, an autonomous AI artist and collaborative creative partner, which was acclaimed in an Artsy editorial as “the biggest artistic achievement of the year.” AICAN art has been exhibited in several art venues in Los Angeles, Frankfurt, San Francisco, New York City and the National Museum of China. In 2021, he led the AI team that completed Beethoven’s 10th symphony, which received worldwide media coverage. He received M.Sc. and Ph.D. degrees in computer science from the University of Maryland, College Park, in 2000 and 2002, respectively.
speakers.name: Dr. Ahmed Elgammal
speakers.organization: Department of Computer Science and Center for Cognitive Science at Rutgers University

description: About the Talk: The use of AI in art making is as old as AI itself. The ways artists integrated AI in their creative process have been evolving with the advances of AI models and their capabilities. What is the value artists find when using AI as part of their process? What is the role of an artist and what is the role of AI in the process? How is that changing now with generative AI being dominated by text prompting as the way of interface? What have we gained and what have we lost with the progress of generative AI taking its course out of the uncanny valley to utility with the introduction of large language models as part of image generation. In this talk I will present my viewpoint about answering these questions as well as feedback from talking to many artists about this issue and trying to understand the ways they have integrated AI in their process.
summary: AI와 예술은 오랜 역사를 함께했습니다. 본 강연은 아티스트들이 AI에서 찾는 가치, 아티스트와 AI의 역할, 그리고 텍스트 프롬프트 기반 생성형 AI가 예술 창작에 가져온 득과 실을 탐구합니다. 다양한 아티스트들의 경험을 바탕으로 이러한 질문들에 대한 저의 시각을 공유할 것입니다.
kor_description:
강연 소개:
예술 창작에 AI를 활용하는 역사는 AI 자체의 역사만큼이나 오래되었습니다. 예술가들이 창작 과정에 AI를 통합하는 방식은 AI 모델과 그 역량의 발전과 함께 진화해 왔습니다. 예술가들은 AI를 작업 과정의 일부로 활용할 때 어떤 가치를 찾을까요? 이 과정에서 예술가의 역할은 무엇이고, AI의 역할은 무엇일까요? 텍스트 프롬프트가 인터페이스 방식의 주류를 이루며 생성형 AI가 지배적인 상황에서, 이러한 역할은 어떻게 변화하고 있을까요? 이미지 생성에 대규모 언어 모델(LLM)이 도입되면서 생성형 AI가 '불쾌한 골짜기'를 벗어나 유용성의 단계로 나아가는 진전 속에서, 우리는 무엇을 얻었고 무엇을 잃었을까요? 본 강연에서는 이러한 질문에 대한 저의 견해뿐만 아니라, 많은 예술가들과 이 문제에 대해 이야기하고 그들이 AI를 자신들의 작업 과정에 통합해 온 방식들을 이해하려는 노력에서 얻은 피드백을 공유할 것입니다.

title: Making AI Work For Everyone

speakers.introduction: Kevin Weil is the VP, OpenAI for Science, previously Chief Product Officer at OpenAI, where he leads the development and application of cutting-edge AI research into products and services that empower consumers, developers, and businesses. With a wealth of experience in scaling technology products, Kevin brings a deep understanding of both consumer and enterprise needs in the AI space. Prior to joining OpenAI, he was the Head of Product at Instagram, leading consumer and monetization efforts that contributed to the platform's global expansion and success. Kevin's experience also includes a pivotal role at Twitter, where he served as Senior Vice President of Product. He played a key part in shaping the platform’s core consumer experience and advertising products, while also overseeing development for Vine and Periscope. During his tenure at Twitter, he led the creation of the company’s advertising platform and the development of Fabric, a mobile development suite. Kevin holds a B.A. in Mathematics and Physics from Harvard University, graduating summa cum laude, and an M.S. in Physics from Stanford University. He is also a dedicated advocate for environmental conservation, serving on the board of The Nature Conservancy.
speakers.name: Kevin Weil

speakers.introduction: Dr. Erik Brynjolfsson is the Jerry Yang and Akiko Yamazaki Professor and Senior Fellow at the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI (HAI), and Director of the Stanford Digital Economy Lab. He is the Ralph Landau Senior Fellow at the Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research (SIEPR) and holds appointments at the Stanford Graduate School of Business, Stanford Department of Economics, and a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER).
speakers.name: Erik Brynjolfsson
speakers.position: Professor of Economics, Operations, and IT

description:
Replay: Learn how AI is reshaping the economy—and why its true impact goes beyond traditional metrics.
Earlier this year, the OpenAI Forum hosted a fireside chat featuring Kevin Weil, Chief Product Officer at OpenAI, and Erik Brynjolfsson, Stanford professor and digital economy expert. They explored how AI differs from past technological revolutions, why its benefits often escape traditional economic measurements, and how businesses can integrate AI to augment—not replace—human workers.
summary: AI가 경제를 재편하고 그 진정한 영향이 전통적 지표를 넘어서는 이유를 탐구한 세션입니다. OpenAI CPO와 스탠포드 교수가 AI의 차별성, 경제적 가치 측정의 어려움, 그리고 인간을 보완하는 AI 활용 전략에 대해 논의했습니다.
kor_description:
다시 보기: AI가 경제를 어떻게 재편하고 있으며, 그 진정한 영향이 기존 측정 기준을 넘어서는 이유를 알아보십시오.

올해 초, OpenAI 포럼에서는 OpenAI의 최고제품책임자(CPO) 케빈 웨일과 스탠포드 교수이자 디지털 경제 전문가인 에릭 브린욜프슨이 함께한 파이어사이드 챗을 개최했습니다. 이들은 AI가 과거 기술 혁명과 어떻게 다른지, AI의 이점이 기존 경제 측정 기준에 잘 반영되지 않는 이유, 그리고 기업들이 AI를 활용하여 인간 노동자를 대체하는 것이 아니라 보강(강화)할 수 있는 방법에 대해 심도 있게 다루었습니다.

title: Careers at the Frontier: Hiring the Future at OpenAI

speakers.introduction: Joaquin Quiñonero Candela is the Head of Recruiting at OpenAI. Previously, he served as Head of Preparedness, where he focused on mitigating catastrophic AI risks. Between these roles, he spent three months coding daily during an internship on AI for healthcare. Joaquin also serves on the Technical Advisory Board of Inditex.
speakers.name: Joaquin  Quiñonero Candela

speakers.introduction: Natalie Cone launched and now manages OpenAI’s interdisciplinary community, the Forum.  The OpenAI Forum is a community designed to unite thoughtful contributors from a diverse array of backgrounds, skill sets, and domain expertise to enable discourse related to the intersection of AI and an array of academic, professional, and societal domains. Before joining OpenAI, Natalie managed and stewarded Scale’s ML/AI community of practice, the AI Exchange. She has a background in the Arts, with a degree in History of Art from UC, Berkeley, and has served as Director of Operations and Programs, as well as on the board of directors for the radical performing arts center, CounterPulse, and led visitor experience at Yerba Buena Center for the Arts.
speakers.name: Natalie Cone

description: This forum conversation showcased OpenAI’s deep alignment between its internal culture and its global mission: to build AGI that benefits everyone. Joaquin’s career story—spanning safety research, a personal return to hands-on coding as an intern, and his unexpected move into recruiting—served as a powerful narrative about humility, mission-alignment, and human-centered innovation. The conversation emphasized OpenAI’s commitment to democratizing AI access, building infrastructure for economic transformation, and advancing AI that reflects democratic values, not autocratic control.
summary: OpenAI는 '모두에게 이로운 AGI 구축'이라는 미션과 문화가 깊이 연결되어 있습니다. 겸손함과 미션 지향적인 인재 양성을 통해 AI 접근성을 민주화하고, 경제 변혁을 위한 인프라 구축, 민주적 가치를 반영하는 AI 발전에 전념하고 있습니다.
kor_description: 이번 포럼 대화는 OpenAI의 내부 문화와 모두에게 혜택을 주는 AGI를 구축하려는 글로벌 미션 간의 깊은 조화를 보여주었습니다. 안전 연구, 인턴으로서 직접 코딩에 다시 뛰어든 개인적인 경험, 그리고 예상치 못하게 채용 분야로 전환한 호아킨의 경력 이야기는 겸손, 미션 정렬, 그리고 인간 중심 혁신에 대한 강력한 서사를 전달했습니다. 이 대화는 AI 접근의 민주화, 경제 변혁을 위한 인프라 구축, 그리고 독재적 통제가 아닌 민주적 가치를 반영하는 AI 발전에 대한 OpenAI의 헌신을 강조했습니다.

title: Scientific Discovery with AI: Unlocking the Secrets of the Universe Key Requirements and Pioneering Projects Highlighting AI’s Contribution to Astrophysical Research

speakers.introduction: Chi-kwan Chan (CK) is an Associate Astronomer/Research Professor at Steward Observatory, University of Arizona, and has been serving as the Secretary of the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) Science Council since 2020. He recently led the publication of the computational and theoretical modeling/interpretation of our black hole, Sgr A*. Professor Chan created EHT's computational and data processing infrastructure and continues to lead it to this day, along with EHT's Software and Data Compatibility Working Group. He is a Senior Investigator of Black Hole PIRE, a leader of the Theoretical Astrophysics Program TAP, a Data Science Fellow, and a member of the Applied Mathematics Program. In addition to pioneering the use of GPU to accelerate the modeling of black holes, Professor Chan also developed many new algorithms to improve and accelerate modern research, built cloud computing infrastructures for large observational data, and applied machine learning algorithms to speed up and automate data processing. Professor Chan has taught and mentored in subjects of machine learning, numerical analysis, cloud computing, and quantum computing, and is an avid hiker.
speakers.name: Chi-kwan (CK) Chan

description: Using the imaging of black holes as a case study, this talk highlights the key requirements for AI to make meaningful contributions to astrophysical research. Dr. Chan introduces several pioneering projects that are integrating AI into astrophysics, covering aspects such as instrumentation, simulations, data processing, and causal inference. He also discusses an innovative project aimed at enabling AI to gain scientific insights independently.
summary: 블랙홀 이미징 사례 연구로 AI의 천체물리학 연구 기여 핵심 요건을 제시합니다. AI는 장비, 시뮬레이션, 데이터 처리, 인과 추론 등 다양한 영역에 통합되며, 나아가 AI 스스로 과학적 통찰을 얻는 혁신 프로젝트도 소개합니다.
kor_description: 블랙홀 이미징을 사례 연구로 삼아, 본 강연은 AI가 천체물리학 연구에 의미 있는 기여를 하기 위한 핵심 요구사항들을 강조합니다. 찬 박사는 계측, 시뮬레이션, 데이터 처리, 인과 추론 등의 측면을 다루며 AI를 천체물리학에 통합하는 여러 선구적인 프로젝트들을 소개합니다. 또한 그는 AI가 독자적으로 과학적 통찰력을 얻을 수 있도록 하는 혁신적인 프로젝트에 대해서도 논의합니다.

title: Event Replay: How People Really Use ChatGPT: The Who, the What, and the How of ChatGPT

speakers.introduction: Aaron “Ronnie” Chatterji, Ph.D., is OpenAI’s first Chief Economist. He is also the Mark Burgess & Lisa Benson-Burgess Distinguished Professor at Duke University, working at the intersection of academia, policy, and business. He served in the Biden Administration as White House CHIPS coordinator and Acting Deputy Director of the National Economic Council, shaping industrial policy, manufacturing, and supply chains. Before that, he was Chief Economist at the Department of Commerce and a Senior Economist at the White House Council of Economic Advisers. He is on leave as a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research and previously taught at Harvard Business School. Earlier in his career, he worked at Goldman Sachs and was a term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. Chatterji holds a Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in Economics from Cornell University.
speakers.name: Ronnie Chatterji

speakers.introduction: David J. Deming is the Danoff Dean of Harvard College, the Isabelle and Scott Black Professor of Political Economy at the Harvard Kennedy School, and a Professor in the Harvard Economics Department. He also served as Academic Dean of HKS from 2021 to 2024.
speakers.name: David Deming

description: The discussion between OpenAI Chief Economist Ronnie Chatterji and Harvard Dean David Deming centered on findings from the largest study to date of ChatGPT usage. They highlighted how AI adoption is broad, fast, and practical, with strong evidence of democratization—closing demographic gaps, benefiting novices as much as experts, and being widely integrated into both personal and work contexts. They also emphasized the productivity and decision-support roles of ChatGPT, pointing toward shared societal benefits and new frameworks for understanding AI’s economic impact.
summary: ChatGPT 사용 연구 결과, AI 도입은 빠르고 광범위하며 실용적입니다. 이는 인구통계학적 격차를 줄이고 모두에게 이점을 제공하며, 생산성과 의사결정 지원을 통해 AI의 민주화와 사회적 혜택을 보여줍니다.
kor_description: OpenAI 수석 경제학자 로니 채터지와 하버드 학장 데이비드 데밍은 현재까지 가장 큰 규모로 진행된 ChatGPT 사용 연구 결과를 중심으로 논의를 진행했습니다. 그들은 AI 도입이 광범위하고 빠르며 실용적이라는 점을 강조했으며, 인구 통계학적 격차를 줄이고 초보자에게도 전문가만큼 이점을 제공하며 개인 및 업무 환경 모두에 널리 통합되는 등 AI의 대중화에 대한 강력한 증거가 있음을 밝혔습니다. 또한, ChatGPT의 생산성 및 의사 결정 지원 역할을 강조하며, 공유된 사회적 이점과 AI의 경제적 영향을 이해하기 위한 새로운 프레임워크를 제시했습니다.

title: Event Replay: Jobs in the Intelligence Age

speakers.introduction: Aaron “Ronnie” Chatterji, Ph.D., is OpenAI’s first Chief Economist. He is also the Mark Burgess & Lisa Benson-Burgess Distinguished Professor at Duke University, working at the intersection of academia, policy, and business. He served in the Biden Administration as White House CHIPS coordinator and Acting Deputy Director of the National Economic Council, shaping industrial policy, manufacturing, and supply chains. Before that, he was Chief Economist at the Department of Commerce and a Senior Economist at the White House Council of Economic Advisers. He is on leave as a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research and previously taught at Harvard Business School. Earlier in his career, he worked at Goldman Sachs and was a term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. Chatterji holds a Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in Economics from Cornell University.
speakers.name: Ronnie Chatterji

speakers.introduction: Mark Murray is Editorial Director at OpenAI, bringing nearly three decades of experience covering American politics as a veteran editor, reporter, and storyteller. Before joining OpenAI, he spent 21 years at NBC News, where he served as Senior Political Editor. In that role, he directed the network’s political coverage, managed NBC’s Political Unit, oversaw its extensive polling operation, and wrote the lead stories on election results and public opinion trends.
speakers.name: Mark Murray

description: Mark Murray and Chief Economist Ronnie Chatterji discussed how AI is reshaping the future of work, focusing on skills, job creation, disruption, and OpenAI’s new certifications and job platform. They highlighted AI’s potential to democratize opportunity, expand productivity, and support students, employers, and employees across industries. The conversation positioned OpenAI as both a researcher and a solution-builder, emphasizing training, accessibility, and economic opportunity.
summary: AI는 업무 미래를 재편하며 기술, 일자리 창출 및 생산성 확장을 이끌고 있습니다. Mark Murray와 Ronnie Chatterji는 OpenAI의 새 인증과 일자리 플랫폼이 기회를 민주화하고 훈련, 접근성, 경제적 기회를 제공하는 핵심 솔루션임을 강조했습니다.
kor_description: 마크 머레이와 수석 경제학자 로니 채터지는 AI가 업무의 미래를 어떻게 재편하고 있는지 논의했으며, 특히 기술, 일자리 창출, 혼란, 그리고 OpenAI의 새로운 인증 및 직업 플랫폼에 초점을 맞추었습니다. 그들은 AI가 기회를 민주화하고, 생산성을 확장하며, 모든 산업 분야의 학생, 고용주, 직원을 지원할 잠재력이 있음을 강조했습니다. 이 대화는 OpenAI를 연구자이자 솔루션 개발자로 자리매김하며, 훈련, 접근성, 경제적 기회를 강조했습니다.

title: Integrating AI Into Life and Work

speakers.introduction: Daniel Miessler is the founder of Unsupervised Learning, a company focused on building products that help companies, organizations, and people identify, articulate, and execute on their purpose in the world. Daniel has over 20 years of experience in Cybersecurity, and has spent the last several years focused on applying AI to business and human problems. Daniel has held senior positions at Apple, Robinhood, IOActive, HPE, and many other companies, as well as consulted for or been embedded in hundreds of others in the Fortune 500 and Global 1000.
speakers.name: Daniel Miessler

speakers.introduction: Joel Parish works on security applications of large language models at OpenAl. Prior to joining OpenAl, Joel spent ten years at Apple working on the red team, on the blue team, and in security engineering for projects like Apple Pay, Apple ID and iCloud Keychain. In his free time, Joel helps track missile proliferation using open source satellite imagery.
speakers.name: Joel Parish

description: In this talk, Meissler shared his philosophy for integrating AI into all facets of life. He highlighted a framework built for leveraging custom prompts as APIs, as well as demonstrated several specific use cases that the speaker hopes will resonate with OpenAI Forum members and translate across disciplines and professional domains.
summary: Meissler는 AI를 삶에 통합하는 철학을 공유하며, 커스텀 프롬프트를 API처럼 활용하는 프레임워크를 강조했습니다. 다양한 실용 사례 시연을 통해 AI 포럼 참가자들이 직업 영역에서 AI를 효과적으로 적용할 수 있도록 돕고자 합니다.
kor_description: 이번 강연에서 마이슬러는 AI를 삶의 모든 측면에 통합하는 자신만의 철학을 공유했습니다. 그는 사용자 지정 프롬프트를 API처럼 활용하도록 구축된 프레임워크를 강조했으며, 스피커가 OpenAI 포럼 회원들에게 공감을 얻고 다양한 분야와 직업 영역에 적용될 수 있기를 바라는 몇 가지 구체적인 사용 사례를 시연했습니다.

title: The Importance of Public Input in Designing AI Systems: In Conversation with The Collective Intelligence Project

speakers.introduction: Saffron Huang is the co-director & co-founder of the Collective Intelligence Project and a technologist, researcher and writer. She was previously a research engineer at DeepMind working on topics such as multi-agent RL and language models, and has worked on technology governance research with organizations including the Center for the Governance of AI, the Ethereum Foundation, Reboot, the Harvard Berkman Klein Center and the British Foreign Office. She co-founded Kernel Magazine.
speakers.name: Saffron Huang

speakers.introduction: Divya Siddarth is the Co-Founder and Co-Director of the Collective Intelligence Project. She is a political economist and social technologist at Microsoft’s Office of the CTO, a research director at Metagov and the RadicalXChange Foundation, a research associate at the Ethics in AI Institute at Oxford, and a visiting fellow at the Ostrom Workshop. Her work has been featured in Stanford HAI, Oxford, Mozilla, the Harvard Safra Center, WIRED, Noema Magazine, the World Economic Forum, and Frontiers in Blockchain.
speakers.name: Divya Siddarth

speakers.introduction: Lama Ahmad is on the Policy Research team at OpenAI, where she leads efforts on external assessments of the impacts of AI systems on society. Her work includes leading the Researcher Access Program, OpenAI's red teaming efforts, third party assessment and auditing, as well as public input projects.
speakers.name: Lama Ahmad

description:
About the Talk:
AI will have significant, far-reaching economic and societal impacts. Technology shapes the lives of individuals, how we interact with one another, and how society as a whole evolves. We believe that decisions about how AI systems behave should be shaped by diverse perspectives reflecting the public interest. Join Lama Ahmad (Policy Researcher at OpenAI) and Saffron Huang and Divya Siddarth (Co-Directors of the Collective Intelligence Project) in conversation to reflect on why public input matters for designing AI systems, and how these methods might be operationalized in practice.
The Collective Intelligence Project White Paper: The Collective Intelligence Project (CIP) is an incubator for new governance models for transformative technology. CIP will focus on the research and development of collective intelligence capabilities: decision-making technologies, processes, and institutions that expand a group’s capacity to construct and cooperate towards shared goals. We will apply these capabilities to transformative technology: technological advances with a high likelihood of significantly altering our society.
Read More About the OpenAI Grant, Democratic Inputs to AI
summary: AI의 막대한 영향력에 따라, AI 시스템 설계 시 대중의 다양한 의견 반영은 필수적입니다. OpenAI와 Collective Intelligence Project 전문가들이 이 중요성과 실제 적용 방안을 논의하며, 변화를 이끌 집단 지능 거버넌스 모델 개발의 핵심을 탐구합니다.
kor_description:
**강연 소개:**
AI는 경제 및 사회 전반에 걸쳐 중대하고 광범위한 영향을 미칠 것입니다. 기술은 개인의 삶, 상호작용 방식, 그리고 사회 전체의 진화에 영향을 미칩니다. 우리는 AI 시스템의 작동 방식에 대한 결정이 공익을 반영하는 다양한 관점에 의해 형성되어야 한다고 믿습니다. OpenAI의 정책 연구원인 라마 아흐마드와 Collective Intelligence Project의 공동 디렉터인 사프론 황, 디비야 시다스와 함께 대화에 참여하여 AI 시스템 설계에 대중의 의견이 왜 중요한지, 그리고 이러한 방법들이 실제로 어떻게 구현될 수 있는지에 대해 논의합니다.

**The Collective Intelligence Project 백서:**
Collective Intelligence Project (CIP)는 변혁적 기술을 위한 새로운 거버넌스 모델의 인큐베이터입니다. CIP는 집단 지능 역량, 즉 공유된 목표를 구성하고 이를 위해 협력하는 집단의 역량을 확장하는 의사결정 기술, 프로세스 및 제도에 대한 연구 개발에 집중할 것입니다. 우리는 이러한 역량을 사회를 크게 변화시킬 가능성이 높은 기술 발전, 즉 변혁적 기술에 적용할 것입니다.

OpenAI 보조금, AI에 대한 민주적 의견 수렴에 대해 더 알아보기

title: The Future of Math with o1 Reasoning

speakers.introduction: Terence Tao is a professor of Mathematics at UCLA; his areas of research include harmonic analysis, PDE, combinatorics, and number theory.  He has received a number of awards, including the Fields Medal in 2006.  Since 2021, Tao also serves on the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology.
speakers.name: Terence Tao

speakers.introduction: Mark Chen is the Senior Vice President of Research at OpenAI, where he oversees advanced AI initiatives, driving innovation in language models, reinforcement learning, multimodal models, and AI alignment. Since joining in 2018, he has played a key role in shaping the organization's most ambitious projects. Mark is dedicated to ensuring AI developments benefit society while maintaining a focus on responsible research.
speakers.name: Mark Chen

speakers.introduction: James Donovan is the Head of Cognitive Outcomes Research at OpenAI. He came to OpenAI having been a founder, VC investor and Partner at Convergent Research; where he helped launch multiple moonshot science organizations including the LEAN FRO (an automated theorem prover for complex mathematics).
speakers.name: James  Donovan

description: During the virtual event on December 3rd, Prof. Terence Tao and OpenAI's Mark Chen and James Donovan engaged in a deep discussion on the intersection of AI and mathematics. They explored how AI models, particularly new reasoning models, could enhance traditional mathematical problem-solving and potentially transform mathematical research. The speakers discussed the integration of AI into various scientific fields, emphasizing AI's role in accelerating discovery and innovation. Key topics included the challenges of AI in understanding and contributing to complex mathematical proofs, the evolving nature of mathematical research with AI integration, and the future of collaboration between AI and human mathematicians. The conversation highlighted both the potential and the current limitations of AI in advancing mathematical sciences.
summary: AI와 수학의 교차점. 테렌스 타오 교수와 OpenAI 연구진은 AI 모델이 수학 문제 해결 및 연구를 어떻게 혁신할지 논의했습니다. AI의 잠재력, 복잡한 증명에서의 한계, 그리고 AI와 인간 수학자의 미래 협력 방안이 핵심 내용입니다.
kor_description: 12월 3일에 열린 온라인 행사에서 테렌스 타오 교수님과 OpenAI의 마크 첸, 제임스 도노반은 AI와 수학의 접점에 대해 심도 깊은 토론을 진행했습니다. 이들은 AI 모델, 특히 새로운 추론 모델이 전통적인 수학 문제 해결 능력을 어떻게 향상시키고 수학 연구에 어떠한 변혁을 가져올 수 있는지 탐구했습니다. 발표자들은 다양한 과학 분야로의 AI 통합에 대해 논의하며, 발견과 혁신을 가속화하는 데 있어 AI의 역할을 강조했습니다. 주요 논의 주제는 복잡한 수학적 증명을 이해하고 기여하는 데 있어 AI가 직면한 과제, AI 통합으로 변화하는 수학 연구의 본질, 그리고 AI와 인간 수학자 간 협력의 미래였습니다. 이 대화는 수학 과학 발전에 있어 AI가 가진 잠재력과 현재의 한계점 모두를 조명했습니다.

title: On Trust: Backdoor Vulnerabilities and their Mitigation with Turing Recipient, Shafi Goldwasser

speakers.introduction: Shafi Goldwasser is Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, and Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at the University of California Berkeley. Goldwasser is also the RSA Professor (post tenure) of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and Professor Emeritus of Computer Science and Applied Mathematics at the Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel. Goldwasser holds a B.S. Applied Mathematics from Carnegie Mellon University (1979), and M.S. and Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of California Berkeley (1984).
speakers.name: Shafi Goldwasser
speakers.organization: Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, University of California, Berkeley

description:
Given the computational cost and technical expertise required to train machine learning models, users may delegate the task of learning to a service provider. We show how a malicious learner can plant an undetectable backdoor into a classifier. On the surface, such a backdoored classifier behaves normally, but in reality, the learner maintains a mechanism for changing the classification of any input, with only a slight perturbation. Importantly, without the appropriate "backdoor key", the mechanism is hidden and cannot be detected by any computationally-bounded observer. We demonstrate two frameworks for planting undetectable backdoors, with incomparable guarantees.
Our construction of undetectable backdoors also sheds light on the related issue of robustness to adversarial examples. In particular, our construction can produce a classifier that is indistinguishable from an "adversarially robust" classifier, but where every input has an adversarial.
Can backdoors be mitigated even if not detectable? Shafi will discuss a few approaches toward mitigation.
This talk is based largely on collaborations with Kim, Shafer, Neekon, Vaikuntanathan, and Zamir.
summary: ML 모델 학습 위임 시 악성 학습자가 탐지 불가능한 백도어를 분류기에 심을 수 있습니다. 이 백도어는 겉으로는 정상 작동하지만, 백도어 키 없이는 어떠한 관찰자도 감지할 수 없으며, 미세한 입력 변경만으로 분류를 조작합니다. 이는 적대적 예제 강건성 문제와 깊이 연관된 새로운 보안 취약점을 제시하며, 이에 대한 완화 방안이 논의될 예정입니다.
kor_description:
기계 학습 모델 훈련에 필요한 막대한 계산 비용과 기술적 전문성을 고려할 때, 사용자들은 학습 작업을 서비스 제공업체에 위임할 수 있습니다. 우리는 악의적인 학습자가 분류기에 탐지 불가능한 백도어를 어떻게 심을 수 있는지 보여줍니다. 겉으로는 백도어가 심어진 분류기가 정상적으로 작동하지만, 실제로는 학습자가 단지 약간의 교란만으로 모든 입력의 분류를 변경할 수 있는 메커니즘을 유지하고 있습니다. 중요하게도, 적절한 "백도어 키" 없이는 이 메커니즘은 숨겨져 있어서 계산적으로 제한된 어떤 관찰자에 의해서도 탐지될 수 없습니다. 우리는 탐지 불가능한 백도어를 심는 두 가지 프레임워크를 보여주며, 각각은 비교할 수 없는 보장을 제공합니다.

탐지 불가능한 백도어 구축은 적대적 예제에 대한 강건성이라는 관련 문제에도 통찰력을 제공합니다. 특히, 우리의 구축 방식은 "적대적으로 강건한" 분류기와 구별할 수 없지만, 모든 입력에 적대적 예제가 존재하는 분류기를 생성할 수 있습니다.

백도어가 탐지 불가능하더라도 완화할 수 있을까요? 샤피가 완화를 위한 몇 가지 접근 방식에 대해 논의할 예정입니다.

이 강연은 주로 Kim, Shafer, Neekon, Vaikuntanathan, Zamir와의 협력 연구에 기반을 두고 있습니다.

title: Unlocking the Economic Impact of AI in Southeast Asia

speakers.introduction: Aaron “Ronnie” Chatterji, Ph.D., is OpenAI’s first Chief Economist. He is also the Mark Burgess & Lisa Benson-Burgess Distinguished Professor at Duke University, working at the intersection of academia, policy, and business. He served in the Biden Administration as White House CHIPS coordinator and Acting Deputy Director of the National Economic Council, shaping industrial policy, manufacturing, and supply chains. Before that, he was Chief Economist at the Department of Commerce and a Senior Economist at the White House Council of Economic Advisers. He is on leave as a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research and previously taught at Harvard Business School. Earlier in his career, he worked at Goldman Sachs and was a term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. Chatterji holds a Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in Economics from Cornell University.
speakers.name: Ronnie Chatterji

speakers.introduction: Roderick Purwana is a Managing Partner at East Ventures, focusing on growth-stage technology investments in Indonesia and Southeast Asia. Roderick is an active investor with a diverse background in operations and investments in the venture capital (VC) and private equity (PE) industries. Prior to East Ventures, he founded and ran SMDV, a technology venture firm affiliated with the Sinarmas Group, one of the leading conglomerate business groups in Indonesia. He spearheaded the creation of EV Growth (which has been merged with East Ventures since 2021) for SMDV, working closely with East Ventures and Yahoo Japan Capital in 2018.
speakers.name: Roderick Purwana

description: The fireside chat with OpenAI Chief Economist, Ronnie Chatterji and Roderick Purwana, Managing Partner @ Each Venture in Jakarta emphasized the transformative economic potential of AI in emerging markets like Indonesia. Chatterji shared insights from his global and policy experience, highlighting the rapid growth of AI adoption among young Indonesians, the scalability of AI-enabled productivity, and the importance of local context in application development. The conversation underscored opportunities in education, workforce development, and AI infrastructure as foundational to unlocking shared benefits and economic growth.
summary: OpenAI 수석 이코노미스트는 AI가 인도네시아 등 신흥 시장에서 엄청난 경제적 잠재력을 지니며, 젊은 층의 빠른 AI 도입과 생산성 확장성이 핵심이라고 강조했습니다. AI 혜택 극대화를 위해 지역 특성을 고려한 적용, 교육, 인력 개발, 인프라 투자가 중요합니다.
kor_description: OpenAI 수석 이코노미스트 로니 채터지와 이치 벤처의 매니징 파트너 로드릭 푸르와나가 자카르타에서 진행한 비공개 대담에서는 인도네시아와 같은 신흥 시장에서 AI가 가져올 혁신적인 경제적 잠재력을 강조했습니다. 채터지는 자신의 글로벌 및 정책 경험을 바탕으로, 젊은 인도네시아인들 사이에서 AI 채택이 빠르게 증가하고 있으며, AI 기반 생산성의 확장성과 애플리케이션 개발에 있어 현지 상황의 중요성을 언급했습니다. 이번 대화는 공동의 이익과 경제 성장을 실현하기 위한 기반으로서 교육, 인력 개발, AI 인프라 분야의 기회를 강조했습니다.

title: AI Literacy: The Importance of Science Communicator & Policy Research Roles

speakers.introduction: Miles Brundage is a researcher and research manager, passionate about the responsible governance of artificial intelligence. In 2018, he joined OpenAI, where he began as a Research Scientist and recently became Head of Policy Research. Before joining OpenAI, he was a Research Fellow at the University of Oxford's Future of Humanity Institute. He is currently an Affiliate of the Centre for the Governance of AI and a member of the AI Task Force at the Center for a New American Security. From 2018 through 2022, he served as a member of Axon's AI and Policing Technology Ethics Board. He completed a PhD in Human and Social Dimensions of Science and Technology from Arizona State University in 2019. Prior to graduate school, he worked at the Advanced Research Projects Agency - Energy (ARPA-E). His academic research has been supported by the National Science Foundation, the Bipartisan Policy Center, and the Future of Life Institute.
speakers.name: Miles Brundage

speakers.introduction: Andrew Mayne is a Wall Street Journal best selling novelist and creator. He was the star of A&E television's Don't Trust Andrew Mayne and swam with great white sharks using an underwater stealth suit he designed for the Shark Week special Andrew Mayne: Ghost Diver. He currently works as the Science Communicator for OpenAI.
speakers.name: Andrew Mayne

description: The Importance of Science Communicator & Policy Research Roles with Miles Brundage, Head of Policy Research at OpenAI and Andrew Mayne, Science Communicator at OpenAI
summary: OpenAI 전문가들이 AI 시대에 과학 커뮤니케이터와 정책 연구자의 중요성을 강조합니다. AI 기술의 책임감 있는 발전과 사회적 수용을 위한 이들의 핵심 역할을 조명합니다.
kor_description:
과학 커뮤니케이터 및 정책 연구 역할의 중요성
연사: 마일스 브런디지(OpenAI 정책 연구 책임자), 앤드루 메인(OpenAI 과학 커뮤니케이터)

title: Technical Success Office Hours (December 2024)

speakers.introduction: Kevin Alwell is a Solutions Architect at OpenAI, partnering with the company’s largest customers to address their most complex challenges using OpenAI’s technology. He recently joined OpenAI from GitHub, where he spent the last six years implementing developer tools at enterprise scale, including GenAI solutions for Software Engineering. Over the last three years, Kevin has spent much of his time serving Financial Services customers. Kevin is based in the Garden State.
speakers.name: Kevin Alwell

speakers.introduction: James is a Solutions Architect at OpenAI, partnering with the company’s largest and most strategic customers to address their most complex challenges using OpenAI’s technology. He has implemented LLM-driven solutions in production, pushing the capabilities of OpenAI’s API across telecommunications, automotive, technology, and other sectors. Before joining OpenAI, James worked in Data Science at McKinsey & Co., where he developed deep learning models for client applications. James is based in San Francisco.
speakers.name: James Hills

description:
The third Technical Success Office Hours, where we tackle technical challenges and share expert insights to support the community. Perfect for those eager to deepen their knowledge and connect with industry experts, this session offers valuable opportunities for growth and collaboration.
Kevin Alwell and James Hills, Solutions Architects at OpenAI, will lead this session as we celebrate the season with cutting-edge AI capabilities designed to enhance your workflows. Dive deep into our latest releases, including exciting year-end surprises that showcase how OpenAI continues to innovate.
summary: OpenAI 솔루션 아키텍트들이 최신 AI 역량과 연말 깜짝 발표로 워크플로우 혁신을 논합니다. 기술적 난제를 해결하고 전문가 통찰을 얻어 지식을 심화하며 성장할 기회를 잡으세요. (96자)
kor_description:
기술적 난관을 해결하고 커뮤니티 지원을 위한 전문가 통찰력을 공유하는 제3회 기술 성공 오피스 아워입니다. 지식을 심화하고 업계 전문가들과 소통하고자 하는 분들께 이상적이며, 성장과 협력을 위한 소중한 기회를 제공합니다.
OpenAI의 솔루션 아키텍트인 Kevin Alwell과 James Hills가 이번 시즌을 맞이하여 워크플로우 향상을 위해 설계된 최첨단 AI 기능을 선보이며 본 세션을 이끌 예정입니다. OpenAI가 어떻게 혁신을 계속하고 있는지 보여주는 놀라운 연말 소식들을 포함하여 최신 릴리스들을 깊이 있게 다루게 됩니다.

title: Expertise, Artificial Intelligence, and the Work of the Future Presented by David Autor

speakers.introduction: David Autor is Ford Professor in the MIT Department of Economics, codirector of the NBER Labor Studies Program and the MIT Shaping the Future of Work Initiative. His scholarship explores the labormarket impacts of technological change and globalization on job polarization, skill demands, earnings levels and inequality, and electoral outcomes. Autor has received numerous awards for both his scholarship—the National Science Foundation CAREER Award, an Alfred P. Sloan Foundation Fellowship, the Sherwin Rosen Prize for outstanding contributions to the field of Labor Economics, the Andrew Carnegie Fellowship in 2019, the Society for Progress Medal in 2021—and for his teaching, including the MIT MacVicar Faculty Fellowship. In 2020, Autor received the Heinz 25th Special Recognition Award from the Heinz Family Foundation for his work “transforming our understanding of how globalization and technological change are impacting jobs and earning prospects for American workers.” This spring, Autor was selected as one of two researchers across all scientific fields a NOMIS Distinguished Scientist. The Economist magazine labeled Autor in 2019 as “The academic voice of the American worker.” Later that same year, and with equal justification, he was christened “Twerpy MIT Economist” by John Oliver of Last Week Tonight in a segment on automation and employment.
speakers.name: David Autor
speakers.position: Ford Professor and Margaret MacVicar Faculty Fellow MIT Department of Economics

speakers.introduction: Tyna Eloundou is a researcher at OpenAI, whose most recent work includes leading safety evaluations, economic impact evaluations, and the democratic inputs to AI grant program. Prior to OpenAI, she worked as an associate economist at the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago and programmer at the RAND Corporation.
speakers.name: Tyna Eloundou

description: About the Talk: Much of the value of labor in industrialized economies derives from the scarcity of expertise rather than from the scarcity of labor per se. In economic parlance, expertise denotes a specific body of knowledge or competency required for accomplishing a particular objective. Human expertise commands a market premium to the degree that it is, first, necessary for accomplishing valuable objectives, and second, scarce, meaning not possessed by most people. Will  AI increase the value of expertise by broadening its relevance and applicability? Or will it instead commodify expertise and undermine pay, even if jobs are not lost in net. Autor will present a simple framework for interpreting the relationship between technological change and expertise across three different technological revolutions. He will argue that, due to AI’s malleability and broad applicability, its labor market consequences will depend fundamentally on how firms, governments, NGOs, and universities (among others) invest to develop its capabilities and shape its applications.
summary: AI가 전문성의 가치를 높일지, 아니면 상품화하여 임금을 저해할지는 핵심 질문입니다. Autor는 AI의 유연성과 광범위한 적용성 때문에, 노동 시장 결과는 기업, 정부 등이 AI 역량 개발 및 활용 방식에 어떻게 투자하는지에 따라 근본적으로 결정될 것이라고 역설합니다.
kor_description: 강연 소개: 산업화된 경제에서 노동의 가치 대부분은 노동 그 자체의 희소성보다는 전문성의 희소성에서 비롯됩니다. 경제학 용어로, 전문성은 특정 목표 달성에 필요한 특정 지식 체계 또는 역량을 의미합니다. 인간 전문성은 첫째, 가치 있는 목표 달성에 필수적이고, 둘째, 희소하여 대부분의 사람이 보유하지 않을 때 시장에서 높은 가치를 지닙니다. AI는 전문성의 관련성과 적용 가능성을 넓힘으로써 전문성의 가치를 높일까요? 아니면 일자리가 순손실되지 않더라도 전문성을 상품화하고 임금을 훼손할까요? 오토(Autor)는 세 가지 다른 기술 혁명 전반에 걸쳐 기술 변화와 전문성 사이의 관계를 해석하는 간단한 틀을 제시할 것입니다. 그는 AI의 유연성과 광범위한 적용 가능성 때문에, AI가 노동 시장에 미치는 영향은 기업, 정부, 비영리 단체, 대학 등 다양한 주체들이 AI의 역량을 개발하고 그 적용 방식을 형성하는 데 어떻게 투자하는지에 근본적으로 달려있다고 주장할 것입니다.

title: The Future of Work Series: The Effects of AI on Talent Management and Workforce Development

speakers.introduction: Aaron “Ronnie” Chatterji, Ph.D., is OpenAI’s first Chief Economist. He is also the Mark Burgess & Lisa Benson-Burgess Distinguished Professor at Duke University, working at the intersection of academia, policy, and business. He served in the Biden Administration as White House CHIPS coordinator and Acting Deputy Director of the National Economic Council, shaping industrial policy, manufacturing, and supply chains. Before that, he was Chief Economist at the Department of Commerce and a Senior Economist at the White House Council of Economic Advisers. He is on leave as a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research and previously taught at Harvard Business School. Earlier in his career, he worked at Goldman Sachs and was a term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. Chatterji holds a Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in Economics from Cornell University.
speakers.name: Ronnie Chatterji

speakers.introduction: Joseph Fuller is a Professor of Management Practice in General Management and Entrepreneurship. He founded and co-leads the school’s project, Managing the Future of Work, as well as the Harvard Project on the Workforce. He currently leads the FIELD Global Capstone course in the first year of the MBA program. FIELD Global Capstone is the school’s premier experiential learning course, in which more than 900 first year MBA students travel to fifteen countries around the world to work directly for local companies on a product or service challenge. He formerly developed the Making Difficult Decisions course in the second year of the MBA program and headed the required The Entrepreneurial Manager course.
speakers.name: Joseph Fuller

description: Joe Fuller, a professor at Harvard Business School, and Ronnie Chatterji, Chief Economist at OpenAI share insights on how AI is reshaping the workforce, emphasizing both the opportunities and challenges it presents. Fuller highlighted the profound impacts of AI adoption across industries, especially in white-collar roles, the shifting skills requirements emphasizing social and interpersonal skills, and the critical role of democratic AI and infrastructure investment for sustained competitiveness and innovation.
summary: AI는 일자리, 특히 화이트칼라 직무를 재편하며 기회와 도전을 동시에 제시합니다. AI 시대에는 사회적, 대인 관계 기술이 중요해지며, AI의 민주적 활용과 인프라 투자가 경쟁력 및 혁신을 위해 필수적입니다.
kor_description: 하버드 경영대학원 교수 조 풀러와 OpenAI의 수석 경제학자 로니 채터지는 AI가 인력 구성을 어떻게 재편하고 있는지, 그리고 이로 인해 발생하는 기회와 도전을 공유했습니다. 풀러 교수는 AI 도입이 산업 전반, 특히 화이트칼라 직무에 미치는 심대한 영향과 사회적, 대인 관계 기술을 강조하는 변화하는 기술 요구 사항을 역설했습니다. 또한, 지속적인 경쟁력과 혁신을 위한 민주적인 AI와 인프라 투자의 중요성을 강조했습니다.

title: Event Replay: Using AI to Navigate Health: A Conversation with Kate Rouch, Nate Gross, and James Hairston

speakers.introduction: Dr. Nate Gross is the VP of Health at OpenAI. He previously co-founded Doximity and Rock Health. He graduated from the Emory University School of Medicine with an MD, Harvard Business School with an MBA, and Claremont McKenna College with a BA in Governme
speakers.name: Nate  Gross

speakers.introduction: James Hairston is OpenAI's Global Head of Innovation Policy responsible for health, devices, and robotics. James previously led Meta's virtual and augmented reality policy team for a decade and was Policy Advisor to the Head of the US Small Business Administration. James serves on the board of the International African American Museum in Charleston, South Carolina and is a proud native of Jersey City, New Jersey.
speakers.name: James  Hairston

speakers.introduction: Kate Rouch is the Chief Marketing Officer at OpenAI, where she is focused on building a global brand that makes AI feel accessible, inspiring, and useful. She believes AI is the most transformative technology of our time and is passionate about helping people understand and engage with its potential.
speakers.name: Kate Rouch

description: In this Forum session, OpenAI leaders Kate Rouch and Dr. Nate Gross joined James Hairston to discuss the launch of ChatGPT Health and OpenAI for Healthcare. They explored how AI can responsibly support patients, clinicians, and health systems by offering personalized, secure, and contextual assistance. Nate emphasized that over 40 million people ask ChatGPT health-related questions daily, highlighting both the demand and responsibility for thoughtful design. Kate shared her personal experience navigating a breast cancer diagnosis, explaining how ChatGPT empowered her to better understand clinical literature, prepare for specialist visits, and communicate with her family. The speakers detailed how ChatGPT Health integrates with medical records, wearables, and health apps to provide a more holistic, patient-centered experience. For clinicians, OpenAI’s tools help reduce administrative burden, align care with institutional policies, and improve documentation. Together, they underscored the importance of trust, transparency, and collaboration in building AI tools that enhance—not replace—human judgment in healthcare.
summary: OpenAI는 ChatGPT Health 및 Healthcare용 AI를 공개하며, 환자와 의료진을 책임감 있게 지원합니다. 매일 4천만 건의 건강 질문에 답하는 ChatGPT는 의료 기록 연동으로 개인화된 도움을 제공, 의료 시스템의 효율을 높입니다. 인간의 판단을 보완하는 AI의 신뢰와 투명성이 강조됩니다.
kor_description: 이번 포럼 세션에서는 OpenAI 리더인 케이트 라우치(Kate Rouch)와 네이트 그로스 박사(Dr. Nate Gross)가 제임스 헤어스턴(James Hairston)과 함께 ChatGPT 헬스 및 OpenAI 헬스케어 출시를 논의했습니다. 이들은 AI가 맞춤형의 안전하고 상황에 맞는 지원을 제공함으로써 환자, 임상의, 의료 시스템을 책임감 있게 지원할 수 있는 방안을 모색했습니다. 네이트 박사는 매일 4천만 명 이상의 사람들이 ChatGPT에 건강 관련 질문을 하고 있다는 점을 강조하며, 이는 신중한 설계에 대한 수요와 책임을 모두 보여준다고 언급했습니다. 케이트는 유방암 진단을 헤쳐나갔던 개인적인 경험을 공유하며, ChatGPT가 그녀가 임상 문헌을 더 잘 이해하고, 전문의 방문을 준비하며, 가족과 소통하는 데 어떻게 힘을 실어주었는지 설명했습니다. 연사들은 ChatGPT 헬스가 의료 기록, 웨어러블 기기 및 건강 앱과 통합되어 더욱 총체적이고 환자 중심적인 경험을 제공하는 방식에 대해 상세히 설명했습니다. 임상의의 경우, OpenAI의 도구는 행정 업무 부담을 줄이고, 기관 정책에 맞춰 진료를 조정하며, 문서화를 개선하는 데 도움을 줍니다. 이들은 총체적으로 헬스케어 분야에서 인간의 판단을 대체하는 것이 아니라 향상시키는 AI 도구를 구축하는 데 있어 신뢰, 투명성, 그리고 협력의 중요성을 강조했습니다.

title: Event Replay: California Teacher of the Year Uses AI to Make Good Teaching Even Better

speakers.introduction: Casey Cuny is the 2024 California Teacher of the Year. Cuny teaches 10th grade honors English and Senior mythology and folklore at Valencia High School in Santa Clarita, California. He serves as English Department Chair and the school’s Instructional Coach. He also teaches at National University in the Masters of Education program working with teachers from all over the country, and he was recently awarded the 2025 National University Academic Excellence Delphi Award. He is also currently working with ASU to develop their new California Teacher Credential program.
speakers.name: Casey Cuny

description: The conversation, featuring educator and California teacher of the year, Casey Cuny showcased how ChatGPT is transforming education. Cuny emphasized AI literacy as a moral imperative, framing AI as an opportunity.  He spoke about democratizing AI benefits, boosting productivity, expanding educational access, and reinforcing the importance of ethical, human-centered use. The dialogue also highlighted teacher adoption, policy considerations, and workforce readiness—areas where democratic AI values, infrastructure investment, and shared benefits intersect.
summary: Casey Cuny는 ChatGPT가 교육을 혁신하며, AI 리터러시를 도덕적 책무로 강조했습니다. AI 민주화, 생산성 향상, 교육 접근성 확대, 윤리적 활용의 중요성을 역설하며, AI 가치, 인프라, 혜택 공유의 교차점을 제시했습니다. 이는 교사 수용, 정책, 인력 준비와 연결됩니다.
kor_description: 교육자이자 캘리포니아 올해의 교사로 선정된 케이시 쿠니는 챗GPT가 교육을 어떻게 변화시키고 있는지 보여주었습니다. 쿠니는 AI 리터러시를 도덕적 의무로 강조하며, AI를 기회로 보았습니다. 그는 AI 혜택의 민주화, 생산성 향상, 교육 접근성 확대, 그리고 윤리적이고 인간 중심적인 AI 사용의 중요성 강화에 대해 이야기했습니다. 이 대화에서는 또한 교사의 AI 채택, 정책 고려사항, 그리고 인력 준비성도 강조되었는데, 이는 민주적인 AI 가치, 인프라 투자, 그리고 공유된 혜택이 교차하는 영역입니다.

title: Event Replay: Careers at The Frontier: Hiring the Future of OpenAI Part 2

speakers.introduction: Red Avila is a Recruiter at OpenAI, where he’s spent the past 5+ years building teams across People, Legal, Research, Engineering, Policy, and more. He’s recruited talent behind our core research and even participated in red-teaming models like GPT-4. He enjoys being a “utility” talent partner, working across different domains and keeping his skills fresh. Outside of work, he spends most of his free time with his longtime partner learning about wine, foods around the world, and using ChatGPT to help him stay fit and active.
speakers.name: Red Avila

speakers.introduction: Sarah Warkov is a Sourcer at OpenAI, where she has spent the past two years building teams across Applied AI and Research. She’s helped recruit engineers behind ChatGPT, Sora, and Operator—often starting from the very first hire—and now supports hiring for OpenAI’s Robotics organization. Before OpenAI, she recruited technical talent at Google, Meta, Amazon, and in startups. Outside of work, she enjoys family time, long walks with her 90-pound dog, and binge-watching reality TV.
speakers.name: Sarah Warkov

description: The OpenAI Forum event, Careers at the Frontier Part Two, spotlighted OpenAI’s hiring philosophy and culture as reflections of its broader mission: building safe, democratic AI that benefits everyone. Speakers emphasized that OpenAI recruits people motivated by curiosity, humility, and concern for safety—qualities that align with advancing AI on democratic values rather than authoritarian ones. The conversation underscored that leadership at OpenAI is hands-on and collaborative, with impact measured not by titles but by the ability to build, scale, and share responsibility.
summary: OpenAI의 채용 및 문화는 '모두에게 이로운 안전하고 민주적인 AI 구축'이라는 미션을 반영합니다. 호기심, 겸손, 안전을 중시하는 인재를 찾으며, 리더십은 협업과 책임 공유를 통해 실제적 영향력을 만드는 데 중점을 둡니다.
kor_description: OpenAI 포럼 행사인 "프런티어 커리어 파트 2"에서는 OpenAI의 채용 철학과 문화를 전반적인 사명, 즉 모두에게 이로운 안전하고 민주적인 AI를 구축하는 것의 반영으로 강조했습니다. 연사들은 OpenAI가 호기심, 겸손, 안전에 대한 우려를 가진 사람들을 채용한다고 강조했는데, 이는 권위주의적 가치보다는 민주적 가치에 따라 AI를 발전시키는 것과 일치하는 자질입니다. 이 대화는 OpenAI의 리더십이 직접적이고 협력적이며, 영향력은 직함이 아니라 구축하고, 확장하며, 책임을 공유하는 능력으로 측정된다는 점을 분명히 보여주었습니다.

title: The Future of Work: A Fireside Chat with Industry Leaders from LinkedIn and OpenAI

speakers.introduction: Aaron “Ronnie” Chatterji, Ph.D., is OpenAI’s first Chief Economist. He is also the Mark Burgess & Lisa Benson-Burgess Distinguished Professor at Duke University, working at the intersection of academia, policy, and business. He served in the Biden Administration as White House CHIPS coordinator and Acting Deputy Director of the National Economic Council, shaping industrial policy, manufacturing, and supply chains. Before that, he was Chief Economist at the Department of Commerce and a Senior Economist at the White House Council of Economic Advisers. He is on leave as a Research Associate at the National Bureau of Economic Research and previously taught at Harvard Business School. Earlier in his career, he worked at Goldman Sachs and was a term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. Chatterji holds a Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.A. in Economics from Cornell University.
speakers.name: Ronnie Chatterji

speakers.introduction: Dr. Karin Kimbrough is the Chief Economist for LinkedIn Corporation. Prior to joining the LinkedIn Corporation in 2020, she served as the Assistant Treasurer for Google and the Managing Director and Head of Macroeconomic Policy at Bank of America Merrill Lynch. In addition, Kimbrough worked at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York as a Vice President and Director for the Financial Stability Monitoring Function in the Markets Group from 2005-2014. She serves on the board of directors for Fannie Mae, is an advisor to 3x5 Partners, and serves on the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago’s Academic Advisory Council and the Economic Advisory Panel of the New York Fed. She holds a Bachelor of Arts from Stanford University, a master’s from Harvard and a Ph.D. from the University of Oxford.
speakers.name: Dr. Karin Kimbrough

description: This discussion between OpenAI's Chief Economist Ronnie Chatterji and LinkedIn’s Chief Economist Karin Kimbrough explores the current and future impact of generative AI on the labor market, highlighting macroeconomic dynamics, evolving skill demands, and global disparities in AI adoption. Kimbrough offers labor market insights based on LinkedIn data, while Chatterji connects these to policy, infrastructure, and human-centered innovation themes promoted by OpenAI.
summary: OpenAI와 LinkedIn 최고 경제학자들이 생성형 AI의 노동 시장 영향, 거시경제 역학, 변화하는 기술 수요, 글로벌 AI 도입 격차, 그리고 정책적 대응 방안을 논의합니다.
kor_description: 오픈AI 수석 이코노미스트 로니 채터지(Ronnie Chatterji)와 링크드인 수석 이코노미스트 카린 킴브로(Karin Kimbrough)의 이번 대담은 생성형 AI가 노동 시장에 미치는 현재와 미래의 영향에 대해 논의하며, 거시경제적 역학, 진화하는 기술 수요, 그리고 AI 채택에 있어 전 세계적인 격차를 강조합니다. 킴브로는 링크드인 데이터를 기반으로 노동 시장 통찰력을 제공하며, 채터지는 이를 오픈AI가 추진하는 정책, 인프라, 그리고 인간 중심 혁신이라는 주제와 연결합니다.

title: Whose Opinions Do Language Models Reflect? Research Presentation by Shibani Santurkar

speakers.introduction: Shibani Santurkar is a researcher at OpenAI working in building safe and reliable machine learning models. Shibani received a PhD in Computer Science from MIT in 2021, where she was advised by Aleksander Mądry and Nir Shavit. Subsequently, she was a postdoctoral researcher at Stanford University with Tatsu Hashimoto, Percy Liang and Tengyu Ma. She is a recipient of the Google Fellowship and an Open Philanthropy early-career grant.
speakers.name: Shibani Santurkar

description: About the Talk: Language models (LMs) are increasingly being used in open-ended contexts, where the opinions reflected by LMs in response to subjective queries can have a profound impact, both on user satisfaction, as well as shaping the views of society at large. In this work, we put forth a quantitative framework to investigate the opinions reflected by LMs -- by leveraging high-quality public opinion polls and their associated human responses. Using this framework, we create OpinionsQA, a new dataset for evaluating the alignment of LM opinions with those of 60 US demographic groups over topics ranging from abortion to automation. Across topics, we find substantial misalignment between the views reflected by current LMs and those of US demographic groups: on par with the Democrat-Republican divide on climate change. Notably, this misalignment persists even after explicitly steering the LMs towards particular demographic groups. Our analysis not only confirms prior observations about the left-leaning tendencies of some human feedback-tuned LMs, but also surfaces groups whose opinions are poorly reflected by current LMs (e.g., 65+ and widowed individuals).
summary: 언어 모델(LM)의 사회적 영향력을 인식, OpinionsQA 데이터셋을 통해 LM 의견과 미국 60개 인구통계 그룹 간 상당한 불일치를 정량 분석했습니다. 65세 이상, 미망인 등 특정 그룹 의견이 LM에 제대로 반영되지 않는 큰 편향성을 확인했으며, 이는 AI 모델의 사회적 합의 및 공정성 확보에 대한 중요한 과제를 제기합니다.
kor_description: 언어 모델(LM)은 점점 더 개방형 환경에서 사용되고 있으며, 주관적인 질문에 대한 LM의 답변에서 반영되는 의견은 사용자 만족도뿐만 아니라 사회 전반의 견해를 형성하는 데 깊은 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다. 본 연구에서는 고품질 여론 조사와 그에 따른 인간 응답을 활용하여 LM에 의해 반영되는 의견을 조사하기 위한 정량적 프레임워크를 제시합니다. 이 프레임워크를 사용하여 저희는 낙태부터 자동화에 이르는 다양한 주제에 걸쳐 LM의 의견과 60개 미국 인구 통계 그룹의 의견 간의 일치도를 평가하기 위한 새로운 데이터셋인 OpinionsQA를 만들었습니다. 모든 주제에서 현재 LM에 의해 반영되는 견해와 미국 인구 통계 그룹의 견해 사이에 상당한 불일치가 있음을 발견했으며, 이는 기후 변화에 대한 민주당과 공화당의 견해 차이와 비슷한 수준입니다. 특히, 이러한 불일치는 특정 인구 통계 그룹에 맞춰 LM을 명시적으로 조정한 후에도 계속 나타납니다. 저희 분석은 일부 인간 피드백 기반 LM의 좌편향 경향에 대한 이전 관찰을 확인할 뿐만 아니라, 현재 LM에 의해 의견이 제대로 반영되지 않는 그룹(예: 65세 이상 및 배우자 사별자)을 드러냅니다.

title: Event Replay:  Understanding the Labor Market Through Real-World Usage Data

speakers.introduction: Alex Martin Richmond is a Labor Economist on the Economic Research team at OpenAI, where she studies AI's impact on the labor market and how AI will transform the future of work. Previously, she was an Economist at the Burning Glass Institute, where she led research on workforce dynamics and labor market trends. She holds a PhD in Economics from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Prior to graduate school, she worked as a research assistant at the Federal Reserve Board of Governors in the Department of Financial Stability. She holds a B.A. in International Studies and Mathematics from the University of Mississippi.
speakers.name: Alex Martin Richmond

speakers.introduction: Daniel Rock is an Assistant Professor at The Wharton School, where his research focuses on the economics of technology, with a particular emphasis on artificial intelligence and its impact on labor markets and organizations. He is also a Co-Founder of Workhelix, a company focused on helping organizations better understand and implement AI in the workplace. Prior to joining Wharton, Daniel was a postdoctoral researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, where he also earned his PhD in Management Science.
